text.title: gemini-api-integration Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for gemini-api-integration. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Select Gemini 1.5 Pro for complex multimodal reasoning, long context (>500K tokens), or code analysis. Select Gemini 1.5 Flash for classification, extraction, and simple Q&A where cost matters more than latency. Select gemini-1.0-pro for text-only legacy integration where 32K context is sufficient. Avoid gemini-1.0-pro-vision; Gemini 1.5 models subsume it.
rule.rationale: Pro handles complex reasoning and cross-file synthesis; Flash prioritizes cost and speed for bounded tasks. The context window is the primary decision factor: tasks exceeding ~100K tokens need Pro; under 100K, Flash is cheaper. Legacy models are deprecated and only used when Vertex AI requires specific model versions.

rule.statement: Use the 1M token context only when the task genuinely requires processing a single large document or codebase whole; do not artificially pad input to justify the model choice. Use native multimodal capabilities (single request with text + image + audio + video) only when all media types are semantically linked; do not mix unrelated video with unrelated text as a way to load both simultaneously. Enable Grounding (Google Search) only when real-time information is required and the user understands the cost impact; do not enable it by default.
rule.rationale: The 1M context is expensive at scale if you do not need it; use Flash for most tasks and reserve Pro for genuinely large documents. Multimodal in one call is powerful but only when the context is coherent (analyzing a presentation slide + speaker transcript + audio + video of the speaker together makes sense; pasting random images into a coding question does not). Grounding costs add up silently and should be transparent to users.

rule.statement: Always check response.prompt_feedback.block_reason before accessing response.text; accessing .text on a safety-blocked response raises ValueError. Wrap access in try-except and log the block reason for audit.
rule.rationale: Gemini's safety filters are aggressive and block responses silently if you do not check. Accessing the text field on a blocked response crashes; the exception is not informative. Logging block reasons helps identify if a class of prompts is systematically blocked by safety filters.

rule.statement: Always configure generation_config.max_output_tokens explicitly; the default is unlimited and causes runaway costs. Set a reasonable bound based on expected output length.
rule.rationale: Without explicit max_output_tokens, Gemini can generate responses with tens of thousands of tokens, multiplying cost unexpectedly. Set 1024-8192 depending on task.

rule.statement: Use gemini-1.5-flash for classification, extraction, and simple Q&A where cost matters more than latency. Reserve gemini-1.5-pro for complex reasoning or contexts exceeding 500K tokens.
rule.rationale: Flash costs approximately 3x less than Pro while maintaining good quality for bounded tasks. Pro's reasoning is measurably better for open-ended synthesis and cross-file analysis.

rule.statement: Enable context caching (genai.CachedContent) when the same large document is queried multiple times in a session; the cache reduces token cost by 75% for cached content.
rule.rationale: Caching amortizes the cost of large document uploads across multiple queries; after two or three questions about the same 1MB codebase, the cache pays for itself.

rule.statement: Use response.usage_metadata to log prompt_token_count and candidates_token_count for every call. Gemini's 1M context makes it easy to accidentally send huge prompts without noticing.
rule.rationale: Logging usage per call reveals cost outliers and helps calibrate prompt sizes. Without logging, a single 500K-token request can blow a budget silently.

rule.statement: When integrating Gemini into an agent, first determine whether the task is multimodal or text-only to select Flash vs. Pro. Then, for file uploads, poll state.name == "ACTIVE" before constructing the analysis prompt. For long-context document analysis, check if chunking is needed (>900K tokens after encoding) or if the full document fits. For function calling loops, cap iteration at a configurable limit (default 10) to prevent infinite tool calls.
rule.rationale: This workflow reduces cost (Flash where possible), prevents async bugs (polling before use), and prevents runaway loops (iteration caps).
